violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Deli made items (i.e. sandwiches, fried chicken) at display have expire dates of 5/31/26. -Majority of items in Deli 2 door cooler are not dated: No open/make date on macaroni and cheese, gravy, deli meats, etc. -Unopened macaroni base with use by date of 05/22/26.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — --Vacuum sealed roast beef was cooked, cooled and vacuum sealed on-site; found for sale at cold display. *Staff removed and disposed of product immediately.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Meat: -Meat grinder parts drying on cloth in meat department. Deli: -Foil used to line oven shelves worn with food spill present (used to catch over flow from cooking roasts).

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Meat -Some dust/debris at walk in cooler fans. -Frost/ice build up around door/shelves and below drain pan of fans. Deli -Small nestle chest freezer with heavy frost build up at interior surfaces. -Splatter/debris at interior surfaces of microwave.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Deli -Grease/residue at hood vents, surround and fire suppression lines above fryers.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Deli was using soapy bucket as sani/wiping bucket. COS-helped Deli staff make and check sani bucket at sani dispenser.--Food contact surface of utensil/equipment was not properly cleaned and sanitized. -Probe thermometer left dirty in water at deli fryer area.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — ---Some of the Deli Express items were not datemarked (i.e. Chuckwagon and Chicken Sandwiches).--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Bags of egg omelets in deli refrigerator were dated 08/20 and 08/26. *Had not been re-dated for thaw date.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Cold rotisserie chicken dated with sell by date of 09/23. -Deli Express on shelf had (discard) date mark of 9/12.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Deli: -Frost buildup at interior of chest freezer in deli prep area. -Food debris on crumbling foil at oven shelves. Meat: -Some dust/debris at fans of Meat walk in cooler.

Demonstration of conformance with variance. — --License holder did not demonstrate conformance with approved variance or HACCP procedures. -HACCP plan logs are not being completed. *Logs printed while on-site.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Properly designed, located, constructed, operated, maintained, cleaned and chemical test kit provided (2 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
